message:
fix: deterministic replay + stale handle correctness bugs

Exhibit 16 (Deterministic Replay):
The demo compared absolute slot numbers between two runs, but the
WASM handle table is global — Run 2 starts from where Run 1 left
off, so absolute slots differ. Fixed by normalizing slot assignments
to offsets relative to each run's first allocation. The LIFO reuse
PATTERN is deterministic even though absolute slots differ.

Updated narrative to accurately describe what's proven: "same
relative allocation pattern" not "byte-for-byte identical."

Exhibit 6 (Stale Handle):
The demo hardcoded status "REJECTED" regardless of the actual WASM
response. If the runtime accepts the close (already-closed handle),
the table showed "REJECTED" but the console showed "accepted" — a
lie. Fixed to show the actual outcome from the WASM runtime.

Full audit of all 21 exhibits confirmed the remaining 19 are
correct. Exhibits 11/19 use manual AbortController/ws.close()
alongside WASM scope.close() which is honest behavior. Chaos test
correctly counts expected errors from cascade-invalidated handles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
